problem:
Find the coefficient of $x^{70}$ in the expansion of
\[(x - 1)(x^2 - 2)(x^3 - 3) \dotsm (x^{11} - 11)(x^{12} - 12).\]

classified_questions.question: What is the degree of the given polynomial?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: To find the coefficient of $x^{70}$, what missing power of $x$ should we aim for?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How many factors do we need to choose from to get a power of $x^{70}$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What are the possible pairs of missing powers of $x$ if we choose two factors?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How do we calculate the contribution to the coefficient of $x^{70}$ for the case where two factors have missing powers of $x$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What are the possible triples of missing powers of $x$ if we choose three factors?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How do we calculate the contribution to the coefficient of $x^{70}$ for the case where three factors have missing powers of $x$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Why are there no ways to get a power of $x^{70}$ if four or more factors have missing powers of $x$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the final value of the coefficient of $x^{70}$?
classified_questions.label: b
